cases.description: Verify Print Preview screen
steps:
1. Select four images. 
2. Tap Next.
 3. Reorder images if required. 
4. Tap Next again. 
5. Verify Back icon. 
6. Verify No Printer Added if printer is not connected or printer name if connected. 
7. Verify Share icon.
 8. Verify collage preview. 9. Verify Copies section with + / - buttons. 
10. Verify AI icon is disabled. 
11. Verify Add Printer button. 
12. Verify Edit icon. 
13. Verify bottom navigation (More, Create, Printer) with Create selected.
cases.expected: Print Preview screen is displayed with all controls.

verifications.description: Verify Print Preview screen
verifications.expected: Print Preview screen is displayed with all controls.
verifications.verification: Manual/heuristic check — review expected result on current screen

cases.description: Media Access Modal
steps:
1.Launch the application.

2.Wait for splash screen to complete.

3.Swipe to continue and complete all onboarding steps (ON_01B through ON_01D).

4.Reach the ON_01E onboarding screen.

5.Locate and verify the "Start" button.

6.Tap the "Get Started!" button.

7.On the home screen, connect the printer, select an image from the gallery, and reach the Print Preview screen.
8.Perform pre-conditions. Observe permission prompt as shown in image_98d7b1.png.
cases.expected: Modal "Allow Sprocket to access photos and videos on this device?" appears with options: "Allow limited access", "Allow all", "Don't allow".

verifications.description: Media Access Modal
verifications.expected: Modal "Allow Sprocket to access photos and videos on this device?" appears with options: "Allow limited access", "Allow all", "Don't allow".
verifications.verification: Verify permission UI: Allow Sprocket to access photos and videos on this device?
verifications.selector: Allow Sprocket to access photos and videos on this device?

verifications.description: Media Access Modal
verifications.expected: Modal "Allow Sprocket to access photos and videos on this device?" appears with options: "Allow limited access", "Allow all", "Don't allow".
verifications.verification: Verify permission UI: Allow limited access

cases.description: Edit & Validation Loader
steps:
1.Launch the application.

2.Wait for the splash screen to complete.

3.Complete all onboarding steps (ON_01B through ON_01D).

4.Reach the ON_01E onboarding screen and tap "Get Started!".

5.Ensure the printer is offline/disconnected, select an image from the gallery, and reach the Print Preview screenPerform pre-conditions. 
6. Apply image edits (crop/rotate/zoom/frame). 
7. Tap "Print" button.
cases.expected: Application displays the full-screen loader (lime-green spinner over 50% opacity #000000 scrim) while validating blockers, then transitions to CR_07A_Print State Transition.

verifications.description: Edit & Validation Loader
verifications.expected: Application displays the full-screen loader (lime-green spinner over 50% opacity #000000 scrim) while validating blockers, then transitions to CR_07A_Print State Transition.
verifications.verification: Manual/heuristic check — review expected result on current screen

cases.description: Blocker Error Handling
steps:
1.Launch the application.

2.Wait for the splash screen to complete.

3.Complete all onboarding steps (ON_01B through ON_01D).

4.Reach the ON_01E onboarding screen and tap "Get Started!".

5.Ensure the printer is offline/disconnected, select an image from the gallery, and reach the Print Preview screenPerform pre-conditions. 
6. While on Print Preview, force a printer error (e.g., manually open the printer door or empty the paper tray). 
7. Tap the "Print" button.
cases.expected: The app displays the appropriate error pop-up (as defined in image_9794c1.png) over the print preview; the print job is blocked and does NOT proceed to the print state.

verifications.description: Blocker Error Handling
verifications.expected: The app displays the appropriate error pop-up (as defined in image_9794c1.png) over the print preview; the print job is blocked and does NOT proceed to the print state.
verifications.verification: Manual/heuristic check — review expected result on current screen

cases.description: Verify AI/Edit, Copy Limits, Print Button and Error Handling
steps:
1. Launch app and complete onboarding.
2. Tap Get Started!.
3. Connect printer and select image from Gallery.
4. Open Print Preview screen.
5. Verify AI and Edit buttons.
6. Change Copies to minimum/maximum and try invalid values.
7. Disconnect printer and tap Print.
8. Reconnect printer and tap Print again.
cases.expected: AI and Edit buttons are visible and respond correctly. Invalid copy values are not allowed and error/limit message is shown if applicable. When printer is disconnected, Print is blocked with proper error message. After reconnecting, Print button is enabled and printing starts successfully. Bottom navigation remains accessible and app remains stable.

verifications.description: Verify AI/Edit, Copy Limits, Print Button and Error Handling
verifications.expected: AI and Edit buttons are visible and respond correctly. Invalid copy values are not allowed and error/limit message is shown if applicable. When printer is disconnected, Print is blocked with proper error message. After reconnecting, Print button is enabled and printing starts successfully. Bottom navigation remains accessible and app remains stable.

cases.description: Verify Save to Gallery option
steps: 3. Connect printer and select image from Gallery.
4. Open Print Preview and apply manual edit (Crop/Edit).
6. In Save Pop-up, tap Save to Gallery.
cases.expected: A "Save as new photo?" pop-up appears over the dimmed screen. The title "Save as new photo?" is displayed in bold, followed by the message: "If you do not save, you will lose any new edits you have applied." The "Save to Gallery" option is selected by default, with "Do Not Save" displayed below it. A Close (X) icon appears in the top-right corner. When "Save to Gallery" is selected, the image is saved successfully to the Gallery, and the user is returned to the previous screen without any errors..

cases.description: Sequential Tips & Tricks Slide Transition Behavior
steps: 3. Reach ON_01E and tap Get Started!.
4. Complete a print job successfully and remain on the Print State Transition Complete screen.
5. Allow the screen to stay idle for 10 seconds without any user interaction.
cases.expected: The next sequential Tips & Tricks item automatically slides in from the right-side panel. The displayed text matches the expected template exactly (e.g., "Creative Idea: Start a 30-day photo challenge and print each day's highlight with your Sprocket Printer.")

verifications.description: Sequential Tips & Tricks Slide Transition Behavior

6. Interrupt app navigation during state transitions.

verifications.expected: The system must NOT display invalid, blank, or conflicting states. Only one valid status is shown at any time: In Queue / Printing / Completed / Paused. No UI corruption or mixed states should appear.

cases.description: Verify Print Queue Does Not Process or Skip Incorrectly During Pause or Error Conditions
steps: 4. Start multi-copy or multi-photo print job.
6. Trigger rapid resume/pause or simulate error condition.
cases.expected: Printing must stop immediately when paused. No further pages or queue items should process until explicitly resumed. Queue must not skip incorrectly or continue processing in paused state.

verifications.description: Verify Print Queue Does Not Process or Skip Incorrectly During Pause or Error Conditions
verifications.expected: Printing must stop immediately when paused. No further pages or queue items should process until explicitly resumed. Queue must not skip incorrectly or continue processing in paused state.

cases.description: Verify access, interruption, and data retention
steps: 1. Launch app, complete onboarding (ON_01B–ON_01E), and tap "Get Started!". 2. Connect HP Sprocket Studio Plus. 3. Select image(s), open Print Preview, and tap Print. 4. Tap the print count box (e.g., "1") to open Print Queue. 5. Disconnect printer during print to trigger interruption. 6. Observe popup; tap "OK". 7. Verify Print Queue state.
cases.expected: The Print Queue opens successfully. A "Printer Disconnected" pop-up appears with the message: "Your printer has been disconnected. Any photos you add to the queue will be printed the next time you connect a printer." The pop-up includes an "OK" button. After tapping OK, the Print Queue status changes to "Paused". The interrupted print job remains at the top of the queue, and all pending print jobs are retained and are not discarded.

verifications.description: Verify access, interruption, and data retention
verifications.expected: The Print Queue opens successfully. A "Printer Disconnected" pop-up appears with the message: "Your printer has been disconnected. Any photos you add to the queue will be printed the next time you connect a printer." The pop-up includes an "OK" button. After tapping OK, the Print Queue status changes to "Paused". The interrupted print job remains at the top of the queue, and all pending print jobs are retained and are not discarded.

verifications.description: Verify access, interruption, and data retention
verifications.expected: The Print Queue opens successfully. A "Printer Disconnected" pop-up appears with the message: "Your printer has been disconnected. Any photos you add to the queue will be printed the next time you connect a printer." The pop-up includes an "OK" button. After tapping OK, the Print Queue status changes to "Paused". The interrupted print job remains at the top of the queue, and all pending print jobs are retained and are not discarded.

cases.description: Verify Queue screen UI and delete functionality
steps:
1.Launch the application, wait for the splash screen, and complete onboarding (ON_01B to ON_01E).
2.Tap Get Started!, connect the HP Sprocket Studio Plus 3.printer, select image(s) from the Gallery, and open Print Preview.
4.Tap Print and wait for the Print State screen to appear.
Tap the Queue/History icon on the top-right corner to open CR_07K.2_Deleting from Queue screen.
1-4: Perform common steps.
5. Verify Back button, Printer name/status, Queue icon and Pause Queue button at the top.
6. Verify Currently Printing and In Queue sections.
7. Swipe left on an item under In Queue and tap the Delete icon.
cases.expected: Queue screen opens successfully. Top options are visible and clickable. Pause Queue works correctly. Queue items are displayed properly. Swiping left reveals the red Delete button and the selected item is removed from the queue. Remaining count is updated. Back button returns to Print State screen successfully.

verifications.description: Verify Queue screen UI and delete functionality
verifications.expected: Queue screen opens successfully. Top options are visible and clickable. Pause Queue works correctly. Queue items are displayed properly. Swiping left reveals the red Delete button and the selected item is removed from the queue. Remaining count is updated. Back button returns to Print State screen successfully.
verifications.verification: Manual/heuristic check — review expected result on current screen

cases.description: Verify updated Print State graphic after queue changes
steps: 1. Launch the application, wait for the splash screen, and complete onboarding (ON_01B to ON_01E).

3. Tap Print and wait for the Print State screen to appear.

6. Verify the Outstanding! message, Queue/Menu icon with badge count, image stack, progress bar, timestamp, status text (Printing) and Done button.
cases.expected: The Print State graphic updates dynamically after queue changes. Deleted photos are removed and reordered photos appear in the correct sequence. Queue badge count updates correctly. Outstanding! message, progress bar, timestamp, Printing status and Done button are displayed correctly and function as expected.

cases.description: Verify "Low Voltage" popup
steps: 9. Select printer HP200 and connect successfully.
13. Tap the Print button to give the print command.
14. Verify the image enters Queue or Printing state and the popup screen appears.
15. Reduce printer battery/voltage to a low level.
cases.expected: "Low Voltage" popup is displayed . The popup displays the bold title "Low Voltage", followed by the message "Low Electric Quantity. Try printing again after resolving the issue." The popup includes an "OK" button at the bottom and a Close (X) icon in the top-right corner. The print job is stopped until sufficient battery/voltage is available.

cases.description: Verify Video Print Preview screen and all UI elements
steps: 1. Launch the application and complete onboarding flow.
6. Verify user is redirected to Video Print Preview screen.
7. Verify first video frame is selected by default with green square border.
8. Select any other video frame and verify green border moves to selected frame.
9. Verify Copies option is enabled and Tiles option is disabled.
10. Verify copies count shows 1 by default.
11. Tap + icon and verify count increases.
12. Tap - icon and verify count decreases.
13. Verify AI Print icon on left side is disabled.
14. Verify Edit icon on right side is enabled.
15. Verify top left Back Arrow and top right Share icon are displayed and clickable.
16. Tap Share icon and verify share options appear.
cases.expected: Video Print Preview screen opens successfully. All UI elements are displayed correctly and function as expected.
